zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Pilkington於大約1341年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾皮爾金頓時，她的父親Sir Roger de Pilkington II51歲，母親Alicia de Bury41歲。她去世於1423年享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pilkington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northwestern): habitational name from a place in Lancashire. The placename may derive from an Old English personal name Pīleca or Pīloc + patronymic -ing + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The surname is established in Ireland, where its presence was first recorded in the early 15th century.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Pilkington was born about 1341, in Pilkington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Roger de Pilkington II, was 51 and her mother, Alicia de Bury, was 41. She died in 1423, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Pilkington est née environ 1341, à Pilkington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Roger de Pilkington II, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Alicia de Bury, avait 41 ans. Elle est décédée en 1423, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Pilkington um 1341 in Pilkington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Roger de Pilkington II 51 und ihre Mutter Alicia de Bury 41. Sie starb 1423 im Alter von 83 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Pilkington è nata il circa 1341 nel Pilkington, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Roger de Pilkington II, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Alicia de Bury, aveva 41 anni. Morì nel 1423, all'età di 83 anni.
